assumptions: The intragroup dividend receivable is a recognised monetary item that gives rise to foreign currency exposure in the consolidated financial statements.
The question is about possible hedge accounting designation under IFRS 9.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge as one of the three top-level hedge accounting models for recognised assets or liabilities and components thereof.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge as a distinct hedge accounting model, including for highly probable forecast transactions and certain intragroup foreign currency exposures affecting consolidated profit or loss.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a separate hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk in consolidated financial statements.
